allSuggestions.reason: This phrase captured the real blocker behind his caution and forum avoidance.

He named the exact next-level shift: owning a decision area beyond delivering design outcomes.

allSuggestions.value: sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set
allSuggestions.reason: This maps sponsorship to the specific forum type that determines org-level influence.
allSuggestions.evidence: “clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set.”

allSuggestions.reason: His growth direction is expressed as a bet with commitment conditions and measurable milestones.
allSuggestions.evidence: “two concrete leadership bets for this half”

allSuggestions.value: milestones…before the next cycle, not only retrospective feedback
allSuggestions.reason: If the plan stays retrospective, it won’t convert leadership bets into visible strategic influence.
allSuggestions.evidence: “milestones…before the next cycle, not only retrospective feedback.”
allSuggestions.better_as: What pre-cycle proof will show progress in the forum before the next cycle?

allSuggestions.value: What kind of air cover would let you take a more visible position?
allSuggestions.reason: It can stay abstract unless it forces decision ownership + sponsorship mapping.
allSuggestions.evidence: “air cover would let you take a more visible position?”
allSuggestions.better_as: For the decision you’ll own, who will sponsor you in the priorities-setting room—and what air-cover commitment will they make?
